jsonLdWeb.name: Rafter Length Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate rafter length from roof span, pitch, and overhang. Includes ridge height and birdsmouth dimensions.

mainEntity.name: How do you calculate rafter length?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rafter length = (half the span) divided by cos(pitch angle), plus the overhang. For a 24-foot span with a 6/12 pitch and 12-inch overhang: run = 12 ft, angle = 26.57°, rafter = 12/cos(26.57°) = 13.42 ft + 1 ft overhang = 14.42 ft.

mainEntity.name: What size rafter do I need?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rafter size depends on span, spacing, pitch, and loads. General guidelines: 2×6 rafters for spans up to 10 ft at 16" OC, 2×8 for spans up to 14 ft, 2×10 for spans up to 18 ft, and 2×12 for spans up to 22 ft. Always consult local building codes and span tables.

A birdsmouth cut is a notch cut into the rafter where it sits on the wall plate. It consists of a seat cut (horizontal, rests on the plate) and a plumb cut (vertical, against the wall). The seat cut should not exceed one-third of the rafter depth.

mainEntity.name: How do you calculate ridge board height?

acceptedAnswer.text: Ridge height = wall height + (run × pitch/12) - (half ridge board thickness × pitch/12). For a wall height of 8 ft with 12 ft run and 6/12 pitch: ridge = 8 + (12 × 0.5) - adjustment = approximately 14 ft from floor.

mainEntity.name: What is the standard rafter overhang?

acceptedAnswer.text: Standard rafter overhang (eave) is typically 12 to 24 inches. 12 inches is minimum for weather protection. 16-18 inches is common for residential construction. Wider overhangs (24"+) provide better rain protection and shade but may require additional support.

mainEntity.name: Do I need a ridge board or ridge beam?

acceptedAnswer.text: A ridge board (typically 1× or 2× lumber) is a non-structural member that rafters attach to; the ceiling joists or collar ties resist the outward thrust. A ridge beam is a structural member that supports the rafter loads, needed when there are no ceiling joists or in cathedral ceilings. Ridge beams must be sized by an engineer.

howItWorks:
How to Calculate Rafter Length

Rafter length is determined by the building span, roof pitch, and desired overhang. The run (horizontal distance) is half the span, and the rafter follows the hypotenuse of the triangle formed by the run and rise.

Rafter = Run ÷ cos(pitch angle) + Overhang
  • Run = Span ÷ 2 (half the building width)
  • Pitch angle = arctan(pitch / 12)
  • Rise = Run × pitch / 12
  • Ridge height = Wall height + Rise
  • Birdsmouth seat cut = wall plate width (typically 3.5" for 2×4 wall)
  • The plumb cut depth should not exceed 1/3 of rafter depth

Example: 24 ft span, 6/12 pitch, 12" overhang: Run = 12 ft, angle = 26.57°, rafter = 12/cos(26.57°) + 1 = 13.42 + 1 = 14.42 ft.
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How do you calculate rafter length?

Rafter length equals half the building span divided by the cosine of the pitch angle, plus the overhang. For a 24 ft span with 6/12 pitch and 12" overhang: run = 12 ft, angle = 26.57°, rafter = 12/cos(26.57°) + 1 ft = 14.42 ft.

What size rafter do I need?

Rafter size depends on span, spacing, pitch, and snow/wind loads. General rules: 2×6 for spans up to 10 ft at 16" OC, 2×8 for up to 14 ft, 2×10 for up to 18 ft, and 2×12 for up to 22 ft. Always consult local building codes and IRC span tables for your specific loads.

What is a birdsmouth cut?

A birdsmouth is a notch cut into the rafter where it sits on the top wall plate. It has two parts: the seat cut (horizontal, resting on the plate) and the plumb cut (vertical). The seat cut width typically matches the wall plate (3.5" for a 2×4 wall). The notch depth should not exceed one-third of the rafter depth.

How do you calculate ridge board height?

Ridge height from the floor = wall height + (run × pitch ÷ 12). For an 8 ft wall with 12 ft run and 6/12 pitch: 8 + (12 × 0.5) = 14 ft. Subtract half the ridge board thickness for the actual rafter seat position at the ridge.

What is the standard rafter overhang?

Standard eave overhang is 12 to 24 inches. 12" is minimum for weather protection, 16-18" is most common for residential construction, and 24"+ provides extra shade and rain protection but may need lookout support for wider overhangs.

Do I need a ridge board or ridge beam?

A ridge board is non-structural — rafters bear against it while ceiling joists resist outward thrust. A ridge beam is structural and supports rafter loads directly, required when there are no ceiling joists (vaulted/cathedral ceilings). Ridge beams must be engineered for the specific loads.

sections.title: What Is the Rafter Length Calculator?
sections.body: The Rafter Length Calculator is a free online tool designed for contractors and DIY builders who need quick, accurate calculations in the construction and building space. By entering your roof span — building width, roof pitch, overhang / eave, you get instant results including rafter length, rafter length, ridge board height. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting rafter length right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In construction and building, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by contractors and DIY builders worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your roof span — building width and need to find the right rafter length. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

faq.question: Is the Rafter Length Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard construction and building formulas trusted by contractors and DIY builders. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What roof span — building width should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate roof span — building width value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Rafter Length Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

faq.question: What does rafter length mean in my results?
faq.answer: The rafter length value represents the primary output of the calculation based on your inputs. Use it as a baseline figure for your construction and building planning and decision-making.
